Description: WTG System ok — Is indicated when the turbine has NO fault.
PLC Flash CardReading — Controller is restarted. The WTG parameters are initialized
PLC Flash Card Reading Error — Controller is restarted. A fault has occurred during initialization of the parameters.
HMI WTG Stop For Grid Disconnection — Is indicated if the turbine is stopped for a planned grid disconnection. This type of disconnection enables idling during the grid disconnection. Brake Prog.: 1
HMI WTG ManualControl — Is indicated when the turbine is in manual control mode.
HMI WTG Manual Stop — Is indicated if the WTG is stopped with an access level >= 270 or if the WTG is stopped via the stop button on the bottom cabinet Brake Prog.: 1
HMI WTG Customer Stop — Is indicated if the WTG is stopped via the visualization with access level <270.
WTG AutoRestart after GridError — Is indicated when the automatic restart is active following a grid fault.
WTG AutoRestart aborted — Is generated if the automatic restart following a grid error or vector shift is not completed successfully.
PLC MemoryReset — Is indicated when the turbine controller is started with the manufacturer parameter settings (e.g. after a software update or replacement of the turbine controller). Brake Prog.: 4
PLC Reboot active — Is indicated when the turbine controller executes a restart (e.g. after grid outages)

Stat WTG FMC-Time After GridFault — Is indicated for a restricted period following a grid error in order to also count the time following a grid error as FMC time. (FMC = Force Majeure Correction, correction when unavailable due to external Brake Prog.: 0 influences) Yaw Prog.: 0
PLC Flash Card Bladedata missing — The alarm is generated when there is not a blade data file for the wind estimator on the PLC flash card. Brake Prog.: 5
PLC Flash Card Bladedata invalid — The alarm is generated when the data file required for the wind estimator on the PLC flash card is invalid. Brake Prog.: 5
PLC TaskCycleTimeViolation — Cycle time exceeded.

SIS EmStop BotBox Activated — Safety chain triggered via emergency off switch on bottom-box control cabinet (power cabinet or communication cabinet for offshore turbines), in connection with FM50. Brake Prog.: 4
SIS EmStop Nacelle Activated — Activation of the safety chain by actuating the emergency stop switch in the nacelle, in combination with FM50. Brake Prog.: 4
MCV EmStop Activated — Signals, in combination with FM50, activation of the safety chain via the main converter.
Vib Y-direction eff. Stop — Is indicated when turbine is stopped due to lateral vibration values being too high.
Vib Z-direction eff. Stop — Is indicated when turbine is stopped due to main shaft direction vibration values being too high.
Vib Y-direction filtered Stop — Is indicated when the turbine is stopped due to vibration values in the tower's natural frequency range being too high in the lateral direction. Brake Prog.: 1
Vib Z-direction filtered Stop — Is indicated when the turbine is stopped due to vibration values in the tower's natural frequency range being too high in the main shaft direction. Brake Prog.: 1
Vib Y-direction filtered Warn — Is indicated when high vibration values occur in the lateral direction in the tower's natural frequency range. Brake Prog.: 0
Vib Z-direction filtered Warn — Is indicated when high vibration values occur in the main shaft direction in the tower's natural frequency range. Brake Prog.: 0
Vib Restartdelay After Vibration — Is indicated when vibrations occur following a quick-stop or storm cut-out. Starting of the WTG will be delayed until this indication is canceled. Brake Prog.: 0
Vib NacelleAccFilt 2nd tower eigenfreq H Warn — Warning that the tower vibrates with its 2nd natural frequency.
Vib NacelleAccFilt 2nd tower eigenfreq H Stop — The tower vibrates with its 2nd natural frequency and the WTG is stopped.
SIS Met WindSpeed WTG Op Active — The alarm is generated if an anemometer fails. Continued operation with a permanently faulty anemometer is possible for max. 28 days. Brake Prog.: 0
SIS Remote Safety-Reset Required — A safety alarm has occurred. Remote reset is possible.
SIS Local Safety-Reset Required — A safety alarm has to be acknowledged on site.
SIS ManStartReqAfterSISAlarm — The alarm is generated when a resettable stop alarm is triggered by the safety system (SIS). A manual restart is required. Brake Prog.: 1

SIS Sen Pepperl+Fuchs Collective Failure — If this error is active, the sensors of the overspeed/tower damper acceleration detection system/temperature limit switch are faulty or one of the detectors is faulty. Brake Prog.: 1
Gen RPM (OpCtrl) H Stop — FM104 is an overspeed detection system sensor stage 1 fault. It is generated directly in the operation control. Brake Prog.: 2
Rot RPM (OpCtrl) H Stop — FM105 is an overspeed detection system sensor stage 1 fault. It is generated directly in the operation control. Brake Prog.: 2
DTR Gen RPM H StopFastIdling1 — The generator speed is too high in status "Fast idling stage 1" .

DTR Gen MCV RPM Diff H Stop — The alarm is generated if the speed difference between the generator speed from the speed monitor and the generator speed from the main converter exceeds a parametrable limit value. Brake Prog.: 2
DTR GenRot RPM Diff (OpCtrl) H Stop — FM110 is an overspeed detection system sensor stage 1 fault. It is generated directly in the operation control. Brake Prog.: 2

DTR Gen Rpm MCV Unplaus Warning — The alarm is generated when the main converter speed monitoring measurement appears implausible for the 10 min mean (deviation from the other two speed measurements). Brake Prog.: 0

DTR Gen Rpm Monitor Unplaus Warning — The alarm is generated when the generator speed monitoring measurement appears implausible for the 10 min mean (deviation from the other two speed measurements). Brake Prog.: 0

DTR Rot Rpm Monitor Unplaus Warning — The alarm is generated when the rotor speed monitoring measurement appears implausible for the 10 min mean (deviation from the other two speed measurements). Brake Prog.: 0
DTR Gen Rpm MCV Unplaus Failure — The alarm is generated when a brief, distinct deviation of the generator speed is detected.
Gen CWPress L Stop — Is indicated when the pressure in the generator coolant circuit is too low.
Gen CWPmp FuseTrip — Is indicated when the generator coolant pump fuse triggers.
GBX OilFilt Alarm(replace Fi) — Is indicated when the gearbox oil filter is to be replaced.
GBX BearTemp 1(Gen) H Warn Lim — The alarm is triggered when the specific generator side temperature threshold P81.00 is exceeded and the turbine is operated with power limiting. Brake Prog.: 0

GBX BearTemp 2(Rot) H Warn Lim — The alarm is triggered when the specific temperature P81.02 for the rotor side gear bearing temperature threshold is exceeded and the turbine is operated with power limiting. Brake Prog.: 0

GBX OilTemp Sump H Warn Lim — The alarm is triggered when the specific temperature P81.04 for the gearbox oil temperature threshold is exceeded and the turbine is operated with power limiting. Brake Prog.: 0
GBX OilPmp Stage1 FuseTrip — Is indicated when the gearbox oil pump stage 1 fuse triggers.
GBX OilPmp Stage2 FuseTrip — Is indicated when the gearbox oil pump stage 2 fuse triggers.
GBX PostCoolingTimeout — Is indicated if after-cooling of the gearbox oil or gearbox bearing is not completed within a defined period when the turbine is at standstill. Brake Prog.: 0
GBX OilLevel 1 L Stop — Is indicated when the gearbox oil level sensor triggers (false alarm possible during the preheating period when the oil is very cold). Brake Prog.: 5
GBX OilPress H Stop — Is indicated when the gearbox oil pressure exceeds the permissible value. Can be caused by blocked or frozen oil cooler. Brake Prog.: 5
GBX OilPress high multiple — Alarm no longer utilized.
GBX OilFilt Warn(75% clogged) — Is indicated if the gearbox oil filter needs to be replaced soon.
GBX OilPress GearInlt L Stop — Is indicated when the gearbox oil pressure is below the permissible value at the gearbox inlet flange.
GBX BypFilterPmp FuseTrip — Is indicated when the fuse for the gearbox oil bypass pump filter has triggered. Bypass filtering is an optional package. Brake Prog.: 0
ALU RotBearContainer Level L Warn — Is indicated when the grease container for lubrication of the main bearing is empty.
ALU RotBearPmp Fault — Is indicated when the grease pump for lubrication of the main bearing does not signal its status correctly (ON/OFF). (ALU= automatic lubrication unit) Brake Prog.: 0
GBX BypFilter replace Fi — Is indicated when the gearbox oil bypass filter is to be checked and replaced if necessary .
ROTB Not Open — Is indicated when the permissible residual rotor brake pressure is exceeded. The brake may be dragging. Brake Prog.: 3
ROTB Wear Warn — Is indicated when the rotor brake pads are to be replaced in the near future.
ROTB Wear Alarm — Is indicated when the rotor brake pads are to be replaced immediately. Starting of the turbine will not be allowed. Brake Prog.: 1
ROTB Temp H Warn — Is indicated when the temperature of the rotor brake pad exceeds the warning value.
ROTB Temp H Stop — Is indicated when the temperature of the rotor brake pad exceeds the cut-out value.

OpC Gen RPM Reduction (SoftBrk)too low — Is indicated when the generator speed does not slow down quickly enough for soft-braking (BP1 or BP5). This triggers a change to fast-braking (BP2). Brake Prog.: 2
Hyd OilLevel L Stop — Is indicated when the oil level in the hydraulic oil tank is too low.

Hyd HydraulicTest cancel — The alarm is triggered if the rotor brake hydraulic test or the yaw system hydraulic brake test is canceled and this is due to a fault of the hydraulic system. Brake Prog.: 2 If another cause is present, FM1500 is triggered. Yaw Prog.: 0
Hyd OilPmp FuseTrip — Is indicated when the hydraulic oil pump fuse triggers.
ROTB AccuR Press L Stop — Is indicated when the pressure in the hydraulic accumulator for the rotor brake falls below the minimum permissible value. Brake Prog.: 3
Hyd OilPmpRuntime long — Is indicated when the hydraulic oil pump cannot increase the oil pressure to operating pressure within the required time. Brake Prog.: 1
Hyd OilPmpRuntime short — Is indicated when the hydraulic oil pump increases the oil pressure to operating pressure very quickly – this indicates a fault in the hydraulic accumulator. Brake Prog.: 1

Hyd OilPmpSwitchingFrequency high — Is indicated when the hydraulic oil pump has to be switched on in quick succession – i.e. the oil pressure increases and falls rapidly. This indicates a fault in the hydraulic accumulator. Brake Prog.: 1
Hyd OilPress H Stop — Is indicated when the maximum permissible hydraulic oil pressure is exceeded.
Yaw HydAccuPress L Stop — Is indicated when the pressure in the yaw system hydraulic accumulator falls below the minimum permissible value. Brake Prog.: 1
HMI ROTB ManualControl — Is indicated when the rotor brake is set to manual control (Service).
ROTB BrakePressure Fault — Is indicated when the brake pressure is not reached on time or is exceeded during an automatic brake test. Brake Prog.: 1
ROTB HoldPressure Fault — Is indicated if the holding pressure does not correspond to the setpoint value after a short period when the rotor brake is closed. Brake Prog.: 1
ROTB HoldPressure Early Warn — Is indicated when the hold pressure is reached too early during an automatic brake test (brake pressure stage II). Brake Prog.: 0
ROTB HoldPressure Early Stop — Is indicated if the holding pressure (brake pressure stage II) is reached much too early during the automatic rotor brake test. Brake Prog.: 1

ROTB AccuR DiffPress — Is indicated when the signals of the pressure switch and the analog pressure measurement on the hydraulic accumulator do not match (plausibility monitoring of the pressure switch and the analog Brake Prog.: 5 pressure measurement). Yaw Prog.: 0

ROTB AccuE PressSwitch Fault — Is indicated when pressure switch E on the accumulator is incorrectly set for the rotor brake stop pressure application delay or the accumulator priming pressure is incorrectly set. Brake Prog.: 2

ROTB AccuE Press L Stop — Is indicated when the pressure in the rotor brake hydraulic accumulator E unexpectedly falls. The control of an important brake valve is monitored for cable breaks by this. Brake Prog.: 3

ROTB HoldPressureSwitch DiffPress — Is indicated if the pressure switch and the analog pressure measurement at the holding pressure switch do not provide matching signals (plausibility monitoring of pressure switch and analog pressure Brake Prog.: 5 measurement). Yaw Prog.: 0
ROTB Test Activ — The hydraulic rotor brake test is active.
MCV CirBreaker Open — Is indicated when the main grid switch (660V grid) is open (turbine class K08 only).
Met Labko Ice sensor CableBreak — The alarm is reported when a cable break or an invalid measured value is detected at the measuring input for the ice sensor. Brake Prog.: 0

Met MoistureSensor CableBreak — Is activated when a cable break is indicated at the measurement input for the moisture or frost point sensor or an invalid measurement value is detected. Brake Prog.: 0
AIS Operation fast idle active — Is reported if the anti-icing system (AIS) has placed the WTG into idling mode.
Met RotAirTemp CableBreak — This warning is generated when a cable break is detected on the temperature sensor TR287 MET Rot Air Temp. Brake Prog.: 0
Gen brushWear Stop — Is indicated if the brush wear warning (FM307) has been active for a longer period of time.
Gen brushWear Warn — Is indicated when the generator slip ring brushes are worn and have to be replaced.
LPR BotBox — Is indicated when the 400V or 660V power supply lightning protection module has triggered.
LPR Gen — A generator lightning protection module on the stator side has triggered.
Met Labko Ice sensor fault stop — The Labko ice sensor signals a fault. The WTG is stopped.
Met Labko Ice sensor fault warn — The Labko ice sensor signals a fault. The WTG is not stopped.
LPR Gen Rot — A lightning protection element on the rotor-side of the generator has triggered.
AIS Enable manual stopp (Service) — Reported if the heating release (ST178) of the anti-icing system (AIS) has been locked manually via the Visu. Brake Prog.: 0
Grid Griderror 660V multiple — The alarm is triggered if a grid fault occurs in the 660V grid several times a day. This alarm deactivates the automatic restart (ARS) function. Brake Prog.: 1

Grid Griderror 660V Alarm — The alarm is triggered if a grid fault occurs in the 660V grid. The turbine is disconnected from the grid immediately and the automatic restart function is activated. Brake Prog.: 2

Grid Phaseshift 660V Alarm — Is indicated when there is a phase shift between the three phases in the 660V grid. The turbine is disconnected from the grid immediately and the automatic restart function is activated. Brake Prog.: 2
LVL InsulatMonitor 660V Stop undelayed — The insulation monitor indicates an alarm (insulation resistance is < 20kOhm).
MVL SF6 Gas Pressure NOK — Is reported if the SF6 gas pressure in the medium-voltage switchgear is NOK.
MVL Switchgear Defect — Is indicated when the MV-switchgear detects a fault that requires a quick-stop (e.g. triggering of the transformer protection or overcurrent protection). Brake Prog.: 2
MVL Switchgear Warning — Is indicated when the MV-switchgear or the MV- transformer detect a fault that requires soft braking.
LVL InsulatMonitor 660V Stop deferred — Is indicated when the insulation monitor generates a warning for a longer period (ST271).
LVL InsulatMonitor 660V Warn — Is indicated when the insulation monitor detects that the insulation resistance from the 660 V grid to ground is too low. Brake Prog.: 0
LVL DiffCurr 400V BotBox Warn — Is indicated when the differential current monitor detects a small fault in the 400V-grid.
LVL DiffCurr 400V BotBox Stop — Is indicated when the differential current monitor detects a serious fault in the 400V-grid.
OBL ObstacleLight Defect — The alarm is triggered if a fault in the aircraft warning lamps is indicated.
Gen Htr FuseTrip — Is indicated when the fuse for the generator heating triggers.
Hyd OilHtr FuseTrip — Is indicated when the fuse for the hydraulic oil heating triggers.
GBX OilHtr 1 FuseTrip — Is indicated when the fuse for the gearbox oil heating elements triggers.
GBX OilHtr 2 FuseTrip — Is indicated if the fuse for the gearbox oil heating elements or, for K08 Gamma CCV, the gear oil and suction hose heating fuse has triggered. Brake Prog.: 0
Air Fan Nacelle FuseTrip — Is indicated if the fuse for the nacelle fan has triggered.
LVL UPS Fault — Collective error message of the USV: battery, temperature, fan or electronics not ok.
LVL UPS BatteryLow — A report is emitted when the batteries of the 230V UPS have a low state of charge.
OBL ObstacleLight Lamp defect — Is indicated when the obstacle lighting is defective.
OBL ObstacleLight Grid Failure — Indicates a power supply failure of the aircraft obstacle warning light system.
Pit error powervoltage supply pitch — The states of the control and feedback of the line contactor and unvervoltage relay are implausible.
Pit PowerVolt SwitchOff manual — Pitch power supply has been switched off manually.
AIS Enable manual active (Service) — Reported if the heating release (ST178) of the anti-icing system (AIS) has been issued manually via the Visu. Brake Prog.: 0
ALU GenBearPmp Fault — Is indicated when the grease pump for lubrication of the generator bearing does not feedback its status (on/off) correctly. (ALU= automatic lubrication unit) Brake Prog.: 0
CMS DTR Communication nok — The Modbus TCP communication between drive train CMS and the operation control has failed.
AIS Modbus Nok — Reported if the Modbus TCP communication between the anti-icing system (AIS) and the operation control is faulty. Brake Prog.: 0
ALU GenBearContainerLevel L Warn — Is indicated when the grease container for lubrication of the main bearing is empty.
LPR Lightning detected — Lightning strike detected.
LPR LightnStrike Sensor Error — Communication fault detected between the lightning strike sensor (Prolec WindSystem) and the control. Brake Prog.: 0
Air Temp Nacelle L Stop — Is indicated if the temperature in the nacelle is below the parameterable limit P10.16 for at least 5s.

GCM GridFrequency Low too long — If the grid frequency is in the monitoring range below the rated grid frequency for too long, the WTG will be stopped with this fault. The alarm starts a recovery period during which the components that Brake Prog.: 1 may have been overloaded can cool down. Yaw Prog.: 0

GCM GridFrequency high too long — If the grid frequency is in the monitoring range above the rated grid frequency for too long, the WTG will be stopped with this fault. The alarm starts a recovery period during which the components that Brake Prog.: 1 may have been overloaded can cool down. Yaw Prog.: 0
Ctrl RPM-number is bad warning — The speed signal for control of the WTG is shown to have inadequate signal quality.
Ctrl RPM-number is bad fault — The speed signal for control of the WTG is shown to have inadequate signal quality.

Grid Frequency notValForStartup — This function prevents the WTG from being connected to the grid when the grid voltage (measured by the main converter, MFR or CWE) is not within the parameterized frequency range. Brake Prog.: 5

Grid Voltage notValForStartup — This function prevents the WTG from being connected to the grid when the grid voltage (measured by the MFR) is not within the parameterized voltage range. Brake Prog.: 5

ROTB Release Check — Is indicated when the rotor brake is closed when the gearbox is very cold. Service/Remote Control should then check whether the brake can be opened again so that the rotor can idle. Brake Prog.: 0
Air Fan TowerBase FuseTrip — Is indicated when the fuse of the (optional) fan in the tower base triggers.
Wind DirSlanting Stop(due YP50) — It is generated if the nacelle has too great a differential angle to the wind direction and cannot be adjusted. Brake Prog.: 1
Yaw ALU ContainerLevel L Warn — Is indicated when the grease container for lubrication of the yaw gear ring is empty.
Yaw Direction <>Cmd — Is generated if the nacelle direction of movement does not correspond to the specified direction of movement. Brake Prog.: 0
Yaw Master Speed Diff Set-Act — Is generated if the yaw master speed difference between target and actual value exceeds a limit value.
Yaw ALU Pmp Fault — Is indicated when the grease pump for lubrication of the yaw gear ring does not signal its status correctly (ON/OFF). (ALU= automatic lubrication unit) Brake Prog.: 0
Yaw MotBrk FuseTrip — Is indicated when the motor protection switch for the yaw motor brake has triggered.

Wind DirSlanting Stop — Is reported if the angle between the nacelle and wind direction has exceeded a parameterable limit that is dependent on the wind speed and adjustment is possible and sector avoidance is not active. Brake Prog.: 5

Wind DirSlanting Stop(at Sectorav.) — Is reported if the angle between the nacelle and wind direction has exceeded a parameterable limit that is dependent on the wind strength and adjustment is possible and sector avoidance is active. Brake Prog.: 1
Yaw Manual Mode Target Position activ — This alarm is triggered when the yaw manual target specification has been activated via NC2.
Yaw MotBrk not closed — Is indicated when the yaw motor E-brakes have not closed within a specified time when the nacelle is braked. Brake Prog.: 5
Yaw BrkResistorTemp Fault — Is indicated when the temperature of the yaw motor brake resistor exceeds a limit.
Yaw HydBrk not closed — Is indicated when the hydraulic brake does not close upon completion of a yaw adjustment.
Yaw HydBrk not open — Is indicated when the hydraulic brake does not open at the start of a yaw adjustment.
Yaw Sliding — Is indicated when the yaw motor speed exceeds a limit during nacelle movement.
Yaw CableTwist LimitSwitch CW Reached — Is indicated if the first nacelle rotation limit switch has been reached in CW direction.
Yaw CableTwist LimitSwitch CCW Reached — Is indicated if the first nacelle rotation limit switch has been reached in CCW direction.
Yaw Axis Test Stop — The yaw axle test was unsuccessful and has been canceled
Yaw ErectJumper Connected — Fault message if a jumper for erection has not been removed.
Yaw HydBrk Test Active — The yaw hydraulic brake test is active.
Yaw MotBrkTest Active — The yaw motor brake test program is active
Yaw HydBrkTest AccuPressLoss — Reservoir pressure monitoring in the yaw hydraulic brake test.
Yaw HydBrk Test AccuPress Low — Reservoir pressure monitoring in the yaw hydraulic brake test.
Yaw HydBrk AccuDiffPress — The alarm monitors the switching point of the system pressure switch.
Yaw Conv 1 Critical Error — The yaw converter 1 reports a critical fault.
Yaw Conv 2 Critical Error — The yaw converter 2 reports a critical fault.
Yaw Conv 3 Critical Error — The yaw converter 3 reports a critical fault.
Yaw Conv 4 Critical Error — The yaw converter 4 reports a critical fault.
Yaw Conv UserMacro Fault — In at least one yaw converter the correct parameter set is not active.
Yaw Conv Local Mode active — Local mode is active for at least one yaw converter.
Yaw MotBrk Test Stop — The yaw motor brake test was unsuccessful and has been canceled
Yaw Conv 1 Cont. CmdOff <> FbOff — The error is indicated if the activation and feedback signals for the contactor of the 400V voltage of YCV 1 is not plausible. Brake Prog.: 0
Yaw Conv 2 Cont. CmdOff <> FbOff — The error is indicated if the activation and feedback signals for the contactor of the 400V voltage of YCV 2 is not plausible. Brake Prog.: 0
Yaw Conv 3 Cont. CmdOff <> FbOff — The error is indicated if the activation and feedback signals for the contactor of the 400V voltage of YCV 3 is not plausible. Brake Prog.: 0
Yaw Conv 4 Cont. CmdOff <> FbOff — The error is indicated if the activation and feedback signals for the contactor of the 400V voltage of YCV 4 is not plausible. Brake Prog.: 0
Yaw Request Axis Test — An axle test of the yaw system is being performed.
Yaw N-1 activation allowed — If an axle of the yaw drive system has failed and N-1 operation can be activated.
Yaw N-1 deactivation — Message for terminating the yaw system N-1 operation
Yaw Multi fault (N-1 not available) — Several yaw axles report non-critical errors:
Yaw N-1 wind speed high — The error is issued if the wind speed is too high during N-1 operation.
Yaw N-1 Time out — The maximum time period during N-1 operation has expired.
Yaw 1 Axis Test Failure — The alarm is triggered if the gearbox test at axle 1 did not complete without errors.
Yaw 2 Axis Test Failure — The alarm is triggered if the gearbox test at axle 2 did not complete without errors.
Yaw 3 Axis Test Failure — The alarm is triggered if the gearbox test at axle 3 did not complete without errors.
Yaw 4 Axis Test Failure — The alarm is triggered if the gearbox test at axle 4 did not complete without errors.
Yaw Parameterset manual command — The parameter sets of the YCV converter can be manually changed via parameters.
Yaw Conv1 DC Voltage Min — The limit value for the direct current link at yaw converter 1 has been fallen below.
Yaw Conv2 DC Voltage Min — The limit value for the direct current link at yaw converter 2 has been fallen below.
Yaw Conv3 DC Voltage Min — The limit value for the direct current link at yaw converter 3 has been fallen below.
Yaw Conv4 DC Voltage Min — The limit value for the direct current link at yaw converter 4 has been fallen below.
Yaw 1 MotBrkTest Failure — The alarm is triggered if the motor brake test at axle 1 did not complete without errors.
Yaw 2 MotBrkTest Failure — The alarm is triggered if the motor brake test at axle 2 did not complete without errors.
Yaw 3 MotBrkTest Failure — The alarm is triggered if the motor brake test at axle 3 did not complete without errors.
Yaw 4 MotBrkTest Failure — The alarm is triggered if the motor brake test at axle 4 did not complete without errors.
Yaw Conv not Operational — The yaw converter does not respond correctly to the commands of the operation control.
Yaw max number ext. Torque reduction drives — The maximum number of the extended torque reduction attempts of the yaw drives has been exceeded. Brake Prog.: 0
Yaw ALU bear pause time H warn — During yawing the pause counter of the ALU lubrication has exceeded the maximum limit value.

Yaw Torque Implausible Alarm — Reported if the deviation of the torque values of the yaw drive axes in relation to each other have exceeded the limit value P77.17 several times or FM494 has already been triggered at least once. Brake Prog.: 0
Yaw Adjustment 90° active — Is indicated when the nacelle is moved 90° out of the wind due to a pitch fault and is adjusted in this position. Brake Prog.: 0
Yaw Conv 1 Error — The yaw converter 1 reports a non-critical drive fault.
Yaw Conv 2 Error — The yaw converter 2 reports a non-critical drive fault.
Yaw Conv 3 Error — The yaw converter 3 reports a non-critical drive fault.
Yaw Conv 4 Error — The yaw converter 4 reports a non-critical drive fault.
Yaw MotBrk 1 not open — Reported if the e-brake of yaw motor 1 has not been opened when the nacelle is moved.
Yaw MotBrk 2 not open — Reported if the e-brake of yaw motor 2 has not been opened when the nacelle is moved.
Yaw MotBrk 3 not open — Reported if the e-brake of yaw motor 3 has not been opened when the nacelle is moved.
Yaw MotBrk 4 not open — Reported if the e-brake of yaw motor 4 has not been opened when the nacelle is moved.
Yaw Speeds not plausibel — Is reported if the deviation of the yaw drive axles to each other exceeds a limit value.
Yaw 0°-RefPoint Diff > Max — Is reported if when a difference between the yaw reference position and the incremental encoder counter becomes too large. Brake Prog.: 0
Yaw Sensorsignal implausible — Is reported if the expected encoder pattern is incorrect.
Yaw referencing — Is triggered if a deviation is detected between the calculated cable twist and the cable twist via incremental encoder. Brake Prog.: 1
Yaw Torque not plausibel — Is reported if the deviation of the torque values of the yaw drive axes to each other exceeds the limit value P77.17. Brake Prog.: 0
Yaw Sliding at Stop — Is indicated when the nacelle slides when the brake is engaged.
Yaw Driven System — It is reported if the nacelle is additionally accelerated by the wind.
Yaw Restrained System — It is reported if the nacelle is braked by the wind.
Yaw failure N-1 switch over — During the change-over to N-1 operation a fault has occurred.
Yaw N-1 not permitted — N-1 operation of the yaw system is no longer possible.
Wind SpeedDiff multiple — Is indicated when the alarm FM501 Wind SpeedDiff Warn occurs four times within 100 hrs.
Wind SpeedDiff Warn — Is indicated when the difference between the left and right hand wind measurement has exceeded a limit. Brake Prog.: 0
Wind Speed (Anemometer) Failure — Is indicated when both wind measurements fail.
Wind SpeedDiff H Warn — Is indicated when the difference between the wind speeds for the left and right hand wind sensors exceeds a limit for a specified time. Brake Prog.: 0
OpC AnemometerTestRotorSpeedMin — The alarm is triggered if a minimum speed is not reached during the anemometer test.
OpC AnemometerTestRotorSpeed Max — The alarm is triggered if the maximum speed is exceeded during the anemometer test.
Wind Speed (backup) constant — Is indicated when the right hand wind measurement is constant.
Wind Speed (primary) constant — Is indicated when the left hand wind measurement is constant.
Wind Speed constant — Is indicated when the value of the left or right hand wind measurement is constant for more than 10 mins. Brake Prog.: 0

Wind Speed Anemometer Ice — Is indicated when there is a deviation between the two wind speed measurements at cold outside temperatures (ice condition deviations). This alarm can be set project specifically via parameter Brake Prog.: 1 P92.03, making a remote reset permissible - or requiring a local reset. Yaw Prog.: 0

Vib Y-Direction Ice — The alarm is triggered when oscillation alarms occur in the Y-direction at cold temperatures (ice condition imbalance). This alarm can be set project specifically via parameter P89.14, making a Brake Prog.: 1 remote reset permissible - or requiring a local reset. Yaw Prog.: 0
Wind DirDiff multiple — Is indicated when the alarm FM541 occurs four times within 100 hrs.
Wind DirDiff H Warn — Is indicated when a difference between the left and right hand wind direction measurements is detected. Brake Prog.: 0
Wind DirDiff H Stop — Is indicated when the turbine stops due to the difference between the two wind direction measurements being too big. Brake Prog.: 1

Wind Dir (Vane) Failure — Is indicated dependent of the wind direction measurement settings when the measurement values for one or both wind measurements is constant or when there is a cable break. The turbine is then Brake Prog.: 1 stopped. Yaw Prog.: 25
Wind Dir (backup) constant — Is indicated when the right hand wind direction measurement transmits constant measurement values.
Wind Dir (primary) constant — Is indicated when the left hand wind direction measurement transmits constant measurement values.
Wind Dir constant — Is indicated when the value of the left and/or right hand wind direction measurements is constant for more than 10 mins . Brake Prog.: 0
Grid ActivePower H Stop — The WTG active power output has exceeded a limit for too long.
Grid ActivePower Peak Stop — The WTG active power output (peak) has exceeded a limit for too long.
Vib AutoTowerFreqMeasure active — Reported if the conditions for the tower natural frequency measurement are met.
Vib TowerFreqMeas implausible — Reported if the measurement of the tower frequency is reported as implausible.
PDiag WindEstimatorTest Error — The alarm is triggered if the anemometer test has not been performed correctly with the wind estimator. Brake Prog.: 5
ALU Gen bear pause time H warn — The pause counter of the ALU generator bearing lubrication pump has exceeded the maximum limit value. Brake Prog.: 0
PDiag activePower<PowerInWind warn — Power output too low at measured wind.
PDiag activePower>PowerInWind Stop — Power output too high at measured wind.
PDiag activePower<PowerInWind Stop — Power output too low at measured wind.
WTG CutIn failed repeatedly — Multiple failure of WTG cut-in.
ALU Rot bear pause time H warn — The pause counter of the ALU rotor bearing lubrication pump has exceeded the maximum limit value.
OpC Operatingstate undefined — Is indicated when a change is made in the program to an undefined status by the turbine control.
Ext WTG ExternalCutOut (DI) — Is indicated when the turbine is stopped due to external conditions.
PLC TimeSynchronisation failed (P26.04) — The WTG control cannot synchronize its time with the Timeserver.
CWE WTG ExternalCutOut — Is indicated when the higher-level farm-controller (CWE) generates the signal to switch off.
CWE WTG FastBraking (CWE) — Is indicated when the CWE/WFM commands a quick-stop (BP6) of the WTG in order to fulfill grid code guidelines Brake Prog.: 6
CWE WTG FastBraking + — This fault occurs when a WTG quick-stop has been carried out in order to fulfill CWE/WFM guidelines.

CWE CommunicationFault Warn — The alarm is generated if there is no Ethernet communication to the higher level farm-RFC (WFM or CWE) and "Warning" is selected as reaction to a communication failure. Brake Prog.: 0

CWE CommunicationFault Stop — The alarm is generated if there is no Ethernet communication to the higher level farm-RFC (WFM or CWE) and "Stop" is selected as reaction to a communication failure. Brake Prog.: 5
Grid too many FRT per day — Is indicated when the FRT day-counter value exceeds the value defined in P13.15.

PDiag rotor ice accretion stop — Reported if in icing conditions an aerodynamic impairment of the rotor blades is present and no anti- icing system (AIS) is installed or it is not operational. This alarm can be set project specifically via Brake Prog.: 1 parameter P54.01, making a remote reset permissible - or requiring a local reset. Yaw Prog.: 0
PDiag rotor ice accretion melt process — Reported if in icing conditions an aerodynamic impairment of the rotor blades is present and a functional anti-icing system (AIS) is installed. Brake Prog.: 1
PDiag EffPower Set-Act Diff > Max Warn — Alarm for monitoring of the difference between the setpoint- and actual active power
CWE Communication Fault Stop Delay — Is set if there is no communication between (WFM or CWE) and WTG for P12.00s and "Stop" is selected in response to communication failure. Brake Prog.: 5

BAO IDD RB Subsystem fault without BP — The rotor blade ice detection (IDD RB) / Blade Additional Option (BAO) reports a subsystem fault without WTG stop. The functionality continues to be available. Brake Prog.: 0

BAO IDD RB WTG State invalid without BP — The rotor blade ice detection (IDD RB) / Blade Additional Option (BAO) reports that the WTG operating conditions for the determination of a state indicator have not been met. The WTG will not be Brake Prog.: 0 stopped. Yaw Prog.: 0

BAO IDD RB WTG State invalid with BP — The rotor blade ice detection (IDD RB) / Blade Additional Option (BAO) reports that the WTG operating conditions for the determination of a state indicator have not been met. The WTG is Brake Prog.: 1 stopped. Yaw Prog.: 0
HMI TestAlarm — Reported if a test alarm is triggered manually via the user interface.
BAO IDD RB Main system fault without BP — The rotor blade ice detection (IDD RB) / Blade Additional Option (BAO) reports an entire system fault without WTG stop. Brake Prog.: 0
BAO IDD RB Ice Warning without BP — The IDD RB indicates minor ice formation on the rotor blade. The WTG will not be stopped.
BAO IDD RB Ice Alarm without BP — The IDD RB reports severe ice accretion on the rotor blade. The WTG will not be stopped.
BAO IDD RB Ice Alarm with BP — The IDD RB reports severe ice accretion on the rotor blade. The WTG is stopped.
BAO IDD RB Alarm terminated — The IDD RB ice alarm has been canceled and can be acknowledged.
BAO Warning without BP — The BAO (Blade Additional Option) reports a warning. The WTG will not be stopped.
BAO Alarm without BP — The BAO (Blade Additional Option) reports an alarm. The WTG will not be stopped.
BAO Alarm with BP — The BAO (Blade Additional Option) reports an alarm. The WTG is stopped.
BAO IDD RB Main system fault with BP — The rotor blade ice detection (IDD RB) / Blade Additional Option (BAO) reports an entire system fault with WTG stop. Brake Prog.: 1
SSF WTG Time Disconnect — Is indicated when the turbine is stopped by a time disconnect due to local time.

Gen WindingTemp 2 CableBreak — Is indicated when a cable break or an invalid measurement value is detected for the temperature of generator winding 2 in the measurement input. Brake Prog.: 5

Gen BearTemp (N-end) CableBreak — Is indicated when a cable break has been detected for the temperature of generator bearing (N-end) in the measurement input. Brake Prog.: 1
ROTB Temp CableBreak — Is indicated when a cable break or an invalid measurement value has been detected for the rotor brake-pad temperature at the measurement input. Brake Prog.: 1

DTR RotBearTemp 1 CableBreak — Is indicated when in the measurement input a cable break or an invalid measurement value is detected for the main bearing temperature. Brake Prog.: 5

ROTB BrakePressure CableBreak — Is indicated when a cable break or an invalid measurement value is detected in the rotor brake pressure measurement. Brake Prog.: 5
GBX OilPress GearInlt CableBreak — Is indicated when a cable break or an invalid measurement value is detected in the gearbox oil pressure measurement at the gearbox inlet. Brake Prog.: 5

Air Temp Topbox PwrFld CableBreak — Is indicated when in the measurement input a cable break or an invalid measurement value is detected for the power cabinet (in the nacelle) temperature. Brake Prog.: 5
Air Temp TransfBox CableBreak — Is indicated when in the measurement input a cable break or an invalid measurement value is detected for the transformer (in the nacelle) temperature. Brake Prog.: 5
Wind Speed (primary) CableBreak — Is indicated when a cable break or an invalid measurement value is detected for the wind speed left measurement. Brake Prog.: 0
Wind Dir (primary) CableBreak — Is indicated when a cable break or an invalid measurement value is detected for the wind direction left measurement. Brake Prog.: 0

PN Measuringbus ComError — The RFC has detected a bus fault in the Profinet module for the measuring bus.
IBS Interbus in Stop (Fast Braking) — Is indicated when a bus fault in the interbus occurs in a delta WTG.
SSF WTG Noise Disconnect — Is indicated when the turbine is stopped by a time-controlled noise disconnect.
Wind Dir (backup) CableBreak — Is indicated when a cable break or an invalid measurement value is detected for the wind direction right measurement. Brake Prog.: 0

GBX BearTemp 2(Rot) H Stop — Is indicated when the temperature of generator bearing 2 has fallen below the cut-out value. Both drive shaft bearings are monitored, the bearing which is further in the gearbox is designated bearing 2 Brake Prog.: 1 (in the direction of the rotor). Yaw Prog.: 0
